Patch Set 23:

two questions:
1. do we really need IT_LAYOUT_MOD? from the implementation, it seems to me that IT_LAYOUT_MOD shares almost all the code of IT_LAYOUT;
2. should we instantiate components for setattr request? I'm not against it but I just want to figure out a good reason by doing so.

Patch Set 23:

Tests uploaded by Trevis Autotest2 from trevis-34vm1 for review-dne-part-2 received by maloo, run on CentOS Linux 7/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/9034c07e-07d2-4051-85ca-09ff8cd01f7b). Ran 15 tests. No failures.

Patch Set 23:

(1 comment)

1. If not use IT_LAYOUT_MOD, it has to be another variable or flag needs to passed through to mdc, most possible be lookup_intent::it_flags, and several places needs to check this flag afterwards, including obd_get_mod_rpc_slot(), and that IMO is not more elegant than a single IT_LAYOUT_MOD intent opc.

2. the design requests the truncate to instantiate necessary components, I have not strong opinion against it. I can think of scenarios to back or against it.

parents.subject: LU-8998 pfl: test cases for lfsck on PFL

commit.subject: LU-9008 pfl: dynamic layout modification with write/truncate
commit.message:
LU-9008 pfl: dynamic layout modification with write/truncate

* in lov_init_composite(), skip init sub object without LCME_FL_INIT
  layout component.
* issue layout intent RPC during write/trunc ops when try to write to
  an un-init-ed component (even if at the lock stage).
* After layout intent RPC issued, restart the IO.
* get rid of unused lov_layout_operations::llo_install() interface.
* add an empty mdt_layout_change() interface to handle intent layout
  write RPC.

Signed-off-by: Bobi Jam <bobijam.xu@intel.com>
Change-Id: I2f79482187d2af2660dd86e55da3f5dc0138e94a
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/25317
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Niu Yawei <yawei.niu@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
